description: Offline-first DataByteSprites lore index for story canon, characters, factions, events, locations, rules, and timeline foundations.

fieldNotes.id: Stable lore identifier used by Studio search, DataByteDex, quests, and future story tooling.
fieldNotes.name: Human-readable lore record name.
fieldNotes.loreType: origin, character, event, location, faction, artifact, rule, timeline, or theme.
fieldNotes.status: canon, active-foundation, design-seed, planned, or locked.
fieldNotes.summary: Short lore summary for search cards.
fieldNotes.preview: Openable path for source or index preview.
fieldNotes.dependencies: Related records required for this lore to work in game systems.

lore.name: The Null Signal Origin
lore.summary: A strange Null signal hits the electrical web of reality and causes DataByteSprites to manifest.
lore.canon: The DataByteSprites universe begins when an unknown Null signal disrupts the electrical web of reality, allowing digital sprites to appear in the real world.

timeline.notes: This is the inciting event for Data Discovery.

lore.name: The Voltaverse Connection
lore.summary: The Voltaverse is the connected layer between real-world electronics, signals, and manifested sprites.
lore.canon: The Voltaverse links the real world to the hidden signal layer where DataByteSprites, Null corruption, and digital forces interact.

timeline.notes: Used to explain why scanners, screens, and electrical systems matter.

lore.name: Vic / V4Victory
lore.summary: Vic is the protagonist, a coder in a messy American room who encounters and captures Leovolt.
lore.canon: Vic, also known by the underground coder alias V4Victory, is the first person to capture a DataByteSprite after the Null signal outbreak.

timeline.notes: Vic captures Leovolt early and brings the discovery to Dennis.

lore.name: Dennis
lore.summary: Dennis is an early support character who helps Vic process the Leovolt discovery.
lore.canon: Dennis serves as a support character in the early DataByteSprites story and becomes part of the investigation after Vic captures Leovolt.

timeline.notes: Vic brings Leovolt to Dennis after the capture.

lore.name: DataByte Coin
lore.summary: The DataByte Coin is the capture artifact used to trap and stabilize sprites.
lore.canon: DataByte Coins can trap, stabilize, and store manifested sprites after a successful capture attempt.

timeline.notes: Used in Vic's first capture of Leovolt.

lore.name: The First Capture
lore.summary: Vic captures Leovolt, proving that manifested sprites can be trapped and studied.
lore.canon: Vic's capture of Leovolt is the first known DataByteSprite capture and becomes the proof that the Null signal outbreak can be interacted with through scanner technology and DataByte Coins.

timeline.notes: This event opens the DataByteDex, scanner progression, and early support-character arc.

lore.name: Screens and Signal Manifestation
lore.summary: Screens, displays, and signal anomalies are major gateways for sprite manifestation.
lore.canon: Sprites often appear through screens, displays, corrupted signal readings, and electrical interference because these are weak points between the real world and the Voltaverse signal layer.

timeline.notes: Supports Screensavior, Monitor Man, DisplayDevil, and Mirror Master lines.

lore.name: NullKing Threat Seed
lore.summary: NullKing is a locked future threat tied to deeper Null corruption and boss progression.
lore.canon: NullKing is not a normal capture target. It represents a deeper Null-aligned intelligence or boss-layer threat that should remain locked until story and mechanics support it.

timeline.notes: Keep locked until boss, admin, and progression systems exist.
